opp.name: Carhartt_S2P_2025
opp.next_step: June 17 Rick: Gartner engaged, then strongly recommended implementing a new procurement solution. However, we face an internal hurdle with their IT department, which heavily favors building custom software over buying established vendor solutions.
opp.ais_why:
• No Avoma calls recorded on the opp; only signal is the SF next step noting Carhartt's 'Procurement Committee continues to develop requirements,' with no AI language.
• Description is blank and there are no transcribed conversations to confirm any prospect-side AI excitement.
• Opp sits at Qualified with steady cadence (last activity May 12) but is committee-led requirements gathering - too early and too quiet on AI to call anything stronger than Curious.

items.quote: Ryan Murphy stated that IT owns procurement technology investments at Carhartt, but no IT budget owner, CIO, VP of IT, or CFO has been identified or engaged.

items.quote: IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying vendor solutions, but no IT leaders have been engaged, and IT's build-vs-buy criteria and decision process are unknown.

items.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, prior sweep memory

sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy (Director, Indirect Procurement)
metrics.narrative: Ryan Murphy articulated qualitative procurement pain on the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call: Carhartt (3,500 employees) lacks a unified P2P platform and relies on fragmented, manual processes that slow sourcing, contract management, and AP workflows. He expressed interest in Zycus iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, and Merlin AI to modernize and automate these processes. However, no quantified business case was developed: no baseline cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or projected ROI/savings were captured. The value case remains entirely qualitative. Without quantification, IT and the Economic Buyer cannot assess the business case against the cost of building vs buying, and the deal cannot progress past Qualified. Quantifying the pain is the immediate next step to justify the $200k investment and counter IT's build preference.

sources: Avoma call history: only Ryan Murphy on 23 Oct 2025 call, no follow-on engagement
Salesforce Contact Roles: Ryan Murphy (Evaluator), Kimberly Blair (Decision Maker, never engaged)
Prior sweep memory: discovery sessions never scheduled, no advocacy evidence
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. Ryan Murphy (Director, Indirect Procurement, Evaluator role) participated in the single discovery call on 23 Oct 2025 and expressed interest in Zycus, but he has not demonstrated any advocacy, access to the Economic Buyer or IT decision-makers, or ability to drive the deal forward. There has been no follow-on engagement with Ryan in 254 days: the planned discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams were never scheduled, the NDA was never finalized, and Ryan has not responded to the rep's 17 Jun 2026 outreach. This suggests Ryan is either unable or unwilling to champion the deal internally, possibly because IT controls the decision and Ryan lacks the organizational influence to counter IT's build preference. Kimberly Blair (Decision Maker, Senior Director Corporate Procurement) has never been engaged, so her support is unknown. No coach, influencer, or internal advocate has been identified within IT, Finance, or senior leadership. Without a champion who has access to the Economic Buyer and IT, can navigate the build-vs-buy resistance, and will drive the deal forward, the opportunity cannot progress. Developing a champion within Procurement and IT is the top priority.

sources: Next Step 17 Jun 2026: IT heavily favors building custom software over buying vendor solutions
Prior sweep memory: in-house PO and supplier setup bots already built, SAP Ariba as potential competitor
competition.narrative: Carhartt faces three competitive forces, with internal IT build preference being the primary and most dangerous competitor. First, IT heavily favors building custom software over buying vendor solutions (source: Next Step 17 Jun 2026), and Carhartt has already built in-house PO and supplier setup bots, creating a status-quo bias and organizational inertia. IT may prefer to incrementally improve these bots rather than replace them with Zycus, and IT's control over procurement technology investments gives them veto power. This is the core blocker preventing the deal from advancing. Second, SAP Ariba was mentioned as a potential competitor (source: prior sweep memory), though no recent evidence confirms active evaluation. Third, the status quo (do-nothing, continue with fragmented manual processes and incremental internal bots) is a competitor reinforced by IT's build preference and the lack of a quantified ROI case. How we win: position Merlin AI (Agentic AI, ANA, Merlin Intake, negotiation agent) as the breakthrough capability that IT cannot build cost-effectively or quickly, and frame the decision as build-vs-buy on AI sophistication and speed to value, not on basic P2P functionality. Engage IT directly with a Merlin-led demo and ROI case showing that building equivalent AI would take 18+ months and cost more than buying Zycus.

sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy (Director, Indirect Procurement)
Next Step 17 Jun 2026: IT build-vs-buy resistance
identify_pain.narrative: Ryan Murphy described specific operational pain on the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call: Carhartt lacks a unified P2P platform, leading to fragmented and manual processes across sourcing, contract management, supplier onboarding, eProcurement, and AP/invoicing. He stated that these inefficiencies slow procurement cycles and create process bottlenecks, and he expressed interest in Zycus to modernize and automate these workflows. However, the pain was never quantified (no baseline cycle time, cost, error rate, or headcount burden), and the pain owner is ambiguous. Ryan Murphy (Director, Indirect Procurement) articulated the pain, but it is unclear whether he owns it or whether IT, Finance, or senior Procurement leadership (Kimberly Blair) are the true pain owners who will drive the investment. Additionally, IT's pain is unknown: the 17 Jun 2026 Next Step reveals that IT prefers building custom software, suggesting IT may not feel the same procurement pain or may believe their in-house PO and supplier setup bots are sufficient. Without engaging IT and quantifying the pain, the pain case cannot justify the investment or overcome IT's build preference.

sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025: mutual NDA requested by Ryan Murphy
Prior sweep memory: NDA never finalized
paper_process.narrative: The paper process is unknown. A mutual NDA was requested by Ryan Murphy on the 23 Oct 2025 call, with Zycus committing to review and return it within 24 hours and the buyer committing to send it. However, the NDA was never finalized, and there has been no follow-on activity on contracting, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, or MSA/SOW scoping. It is unclear whether Carhartt requires an RFP, a formal vendor onboarding process, or InfoSec/data privacy review before contracting. Additionally, IT's involvement in the paper process is unknown: if IT controls procurement technology investments, IT may need to approve architecture, integrations, and security before Legal can proceed. No current contract expiration or replacement timeline has been captured, and no SI/partner involvement has been discussed. The paper process will remain invisible until the deal restarts and advances past discovery into a formal evaluation.

sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy statement that IT owns procurement technology investments
Salesforce Contact Roles: Kimberly Blair (Decision Maker) never engaged
Next Step 17 Jun 2026: IT department controls build-vs-buy decision
economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is unknown and unengaged. Ryan Murphy stated on the 23 Oct 2025 call that IT owns procurement technology investments at Carhartt, but no IT budget owner, CIO, VP of IT, or CFO has been identified, engaged, or appeared on any call or Salesforce activity. The only mapped Decision Maker is Kimberly Blair (Senior Director, Corporate Procurement), but she has never been engaged; her support is unconfirmed, and it is unclear whether she controls budget or merely procurement process. With IT driving the build-vs-buy decision per the 17 Jun 2026 Next Step, the true Economic Buyer is likely an IT executive or the CFO, and they remain completely unmapped. Without identifying and engaging the Economic Buyer, the deal cannot secure budget approval, address the IT build preference, or move forward. Mapping the IT budget owner is the single most critical action.

sources: Avoma call history: only Ryan Murphy on 23 Oct 2025 call
Salesforce Contact Roles: no IT, Finance, or C-level contacts engaged
Next Step 17 Jun 2026: Gartner engagement mentioned but no process detail
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. The single discovery call on 23 Oct 2025 involved only Ryan Murphy (Director, Indirect Procurement, Evaluator role). No other stakeholders have appeared: Kimberly Blair (Decision Maker, Senior Director Corporate Procurement) has never been engaged, no IT leaders have joined, and no Economic Buyer or executive sponsor has surfaced. The planned discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams were never scheduled, so the evaluation has not begun. The 17 Jun 2026 Next Step mentions that Gartner recommended implementing a new procurement solution, but it is unclear whether Carhartt has initiated a formal RFI/RFP, a vendor selection process, or any structured evaluation. IT's build-vs-buy decision process is entirely opaque: we do not know who makes that call, what the timeline is, or whether IT will even consider vendor platforms. The approval chain is also unknown: it is unclear whether Procurement, IT, Finance, or the C-suite must sign off, and in what order. Without a mapped process, the deal cannot be navigated or forecasted.

sources: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy high-level functional needs
Prior sweep memory: discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams never scheduled
decision_criteria.narrative: Ryan Murphy outlined high-level functional needs on the 23 Oct 2025 call: a unified P2P platform covering contract management (iContract), sourcing (iSource), supplier management (iSupplier), eProcurement, eInvoicing, and AI-driven automation (Merlin). He expressed strong interest in Merlin AI capabilities for intake, negotiation, and analytics (Agentic AI, ANA). However, no formal evaluation criteria, scoring model, or decision framework was discussed. The planned discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams to surface detailed pain points and requirements were never scheduled, so the functional, technical, and integration criteria remain uncaptured. Additionally, IT's build-vs-buy criteria are unknown: we do not know what would make IT prefer buying over building, or how they will score vendor platforms against custom development. Without these criteria, Zycus cannot tailor the value proposition, position Merlin as the decisive differentiator, or address IT's objections. Capturing formal evaluation criteria from both Procurement and IT is essential to move past Qualified.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — No AIS (AI Score/Status/Why) fields are populated, so the AI fit signal is unknown from Salesforce. However, Ryan Murphy's strong interest in Merlin AI (intake automation, agentic negotiation, advanced analytics) on the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call suggests AI Curious or AI Hungry appetite at the Procurement level. The 17 Jun 2026 Next Step mentions Gartner's recommendation to implement a new procurement solution, which may signal openness to modern AI-driven platforms. However, IT's build-vs-buy preference and existing in-house bots suggest IT may view AI as something they can build incrementally rather than buy, which would lower the AI fit signal at the decision-making level. The true AI fit is ambiguous until IT is engaged and their AI appetite is assessed.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — No AIS (AI Score/Status/Why) fields are populated, so the AI fit signal is unknown from Salesforce.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Merlin AI is included in the product scope (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA), and Ryan Murphy expressed strong interest in Merlin AI capabilities on the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call. However, the AI positioning has not been activated: IT has never been shown the Merlin value proposition, no AI-led demo or ROI workshop has been conducted, and the narrative that Merlin AI is the breakthrough capability IT cannot cost-effectively build has not been delivered. The deal is under-positioned on AI because the primary decision-maker (IT) is unengaged and the AI differentiation has not been leveraged to counter the build-vs-buy resistance.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 15 is carried by momentum 60 vs stage-expected 50; competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 60 (moving forward): Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.90).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 44 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 4 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 6 is anchored on the stage win (15), nudged by commitment 44 and momentum 60, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls).
Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

However, IT's build-vs-buy preference and existing in-house bots suggest IT may view AI as something they can build incrementally rather than buy, which would lower the AI fit signal at the decision-making level. Rep-initiated outreach per Next Step: Gartner engaged and recommended implementing a new procurement solution; IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying vendor solutions, creating an internal hurdle; awaiting buyer reply

deal_movement.summary: The deal has been stalled in Qualified stage for 254 days (8.4 months) since creation on 23 Oct 2025, with no stage progression, no buyer engagement since the initial discovery call, and no follow-on activity on the planned discovery sessions or NDA. The only recent activity is a rep-initiated outreach on 17 Jun 2026 noting Gartner's recommendation and IT's build-vs-buy resistance, but this is awaiting buyer reply and does not constitute buyer engagement. The deal is effectively dormant.

items.detail: IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying vendor solutions, creating an organizational blocker to adopting Zycus or any external platform. This is the primary vulnerability preventing the deal from advancing past Qualified.

Economic Buyer is unknown and unengaged. IT owns procurement technology investments per Ryan Murphy (23 Oct 2025), but no IT budget owner has been identified or engaged. Decision Maker Kimberly Blair (Senior Director, Corporate Procurement) has never appeared on a call or activity, so her support is unconfirmed. Without IT and senior Procurement alignment, the deal cannot move forward.

Budget constraints exist due to Carhartt's size (3,500 employees), and IT controls procurement technology spend. No budget owner identified, no budget validation completed, no ROI case quantified. The deal amount ($200k) is sized but not validated against IT's budget or competing build-vs-buy allocation.

The deal has been stalled in Qualified stage for 247 days (8+ months) with no follow-on buyer engagement since the initial discovery call. The planned discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams were never scheduled, and the NDA was never finalized. The close date (12 Nov 2026, 139 days out) is indefensible without an immediate restart and accelerated path through the remaining gates.

Carhartt has already built in-house PO and supplier setup bots, which IT may prefer to incrementally improve rather than replace with a vendor platform. This creates inertia and a status-quo bias that reinforces IT's build-over-buy preference.

The deal has been stalled in Qualified stage for 254 days (8+ months) with no follow-on buyer engagement since the initial discovery call. The planned discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams were never scheduled, and the NDA was never finalized. The close date (12 Nov 2026, 131 days out) is indefensible without an immediate restart and accelerated path through the remaining gates.

items.action: Engage IT leadership directly to map the Economic Buyer, understand IT's build-vs-buy criteria and decision process, and position Merlin AI as the breakthrough capability IT cannot cost-effectively build; deliver a Merlin-led demo and ROI case showing that building equivalent AI would take 18+ months and cost more than buying Zycus.
items.trigger: IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying vendor solutions, but no IT budget owner or decision-maker has been identified or engaged; IT controls procurement technology investments per Ryan Murphy.
items.expected_effect: Maps the Economic Buyer, addresses the primary organizational blocker (IT build-vs-buy resistance), and positions Merlin AI as the decisive differentiator; without this, the deal cannot progress past Qualified or secure budget approval.

items.action: Re-engage Ryan Murphy and escalate to Kimberly Blair (Decision Maker, Senior Director Corporate Procurement) to confirm Procurement's support, assess whether the deal is still a priority, and secure their sponsorship for IT engagement; if no response within 7 days, disqualify the opportunity.
items.trigger: Ryan Murphy (Evaluator) has not engaged in 254 days since the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call; Kimberly Blair (Decision Maker) has never been engaged; rep outreach on 17 Jun 2026 is awaiting buyer reply.
items.expected_effect: Restarts buyer engagement, confirms whether Procurement will sponsor the deal, and assesses whether the opportunity is still viable; if Procurement is unresponsive, disqualify and reallocate resources to live deals.

items.action: Build and deliver a quantified ROI case showing baseline cost, cycle time, and manual effort of Carhartt's current fragmented P2P processes vs Zycus, with projected savings, efficiency gains, and AI-driven value (Merlin negotiation, intake automation, analytics); use this to justify the $200k investment and counter IT's build-vs-buy preference.
items.trigger: No quantified business case, baseline cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or projected ROI/savings were captured on the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call; without quantification, IT and the Economic Buyer cannot assess the business case.
items.expected_effect: Provides the financial justification for buying vs building, strengthens the business case, and arms Procurement and IT with the data needed to secure budget approval and overcome status-quo bias.

items.action: Develop a champion within Procurement (Ryan Murphy or Kimberly Blair) or IT who has access to the Economic Buyer, can navigate the build-vs-buy resistance, and will drive the deal forward internally; if no champion can be identified, escalate to the deal owner's manager for executive alignment.
items.trigger: No champion has been identified or developed; Ryan Murphy has not demonstrated advocacy or access to decision-makers; Kimberly Blair has never been engaged.
items.expected_effect: Establishes an internal advocate who can navigate the organizational politics, access the Economic Buyer, and push the deal through IT's resistance; without a champion, the deal cannot progress or close.

items.action: Leverage Gartner's recommendation (mentioned in the 17 Jun 2026 Next Step) as third-party validation to counter IT's build-vs-buy preference; request a joint Gartner-Zycus session with IT to present the analyst perspective on build vs buy for modern AI-driven procurement platforms.
items.trigger: Gartner engaged and strongly recommended implementing a new procurement solution, but IT's build-vs-buy resistance remains unaddressed.
items.expected_effect: Brings third-party credibility and analyst perspective to the build-vs-buy decision, reducing IT's perceived risk of buying and increasing the likelihood of vendor platform consideration.

confidence_signals.summary: The deal is dormant and at extreme risk. There has been no two-way buyer engagement in 254 days (since the single discovery call on 23 Oct 2025), no champion has been developed, the Economic Buyer is unmapped and unengaged, IT's build-vs-buy resistance is unresolved, and the deal is single-threaded on an Evaluator (Ryan Murphy) who has gone silent. The rep's 17 Jun 2026 outreach noted the IT blocker but is awaiting buyer reply. The close date (131 days) is indefensible without immediate IT and senior Procurement engagement. Without a restart in the next 7 to 14 days, this deal should be disqualified.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 131. Time in Qualified stage: 254 days (8.4 months) since creation 23 Oct 2025, with no stage progression. No qualified_date stamp or formal evaluation/shortlist submission dates recorded. Last verified buyer engagement: initial discovery call 23 Oct 2025 (254 days ago). Last Salesforce activity: 17 Jun 2026 (rep-initiated outreach per Next Step, awaiting buyer reply, 17 days ago). Required pace to close by 12 Nov 2026: the deal must move from Qualified → Formal Evaluation → Shortlisted → Vendor Selected → Negotiation → Contract in 131 days, requiring stage progression every 26 days on average. Actual pace: zero stage movement in 254 days. Forward slip risk: none recorded in field history, but the close date is aspirational given zero traction. The deal has been effectively dormant since the single October 2025 discovery call.
evidence: Stage has been Qualified since creation on 23 Oct 2025, with no progression in 254 days (source: Salesforce CreatedDate, LastModifiedDate, StageName).
Last verified two-way buyer engagement: initial discovery call 23 Oct 2025 with Ryan Murphy, 254 days ago (source: Avoma call 23 Oct 2025).

Rep-initiated outreach 17 Jun 2026 per Next Step noting Gartner engagement and IT build-vs-buy resistance; this is a rep touch awaiting buyer reply, not buyer engagement (source: Salesforce Next Step, 17 Jun 2026).
IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying vendor solutions, creating an organizational blocker that remains unaddressed (source: Next Step 17 Jun 2026).

Economic Buyer unknown and unengaged; IT owns procurement technology investments per Ryan Murphy on the Oct 2025 call, but no IT budget owner has been identified or engaged (source: Avoma call 23 Oct 2025, Salesforce Contact Roles).

Decision Maker Kimberly Blair (Senior Director, Corporate Procurement) has never appeared on a call or Salesforce activity, so her support is unconfirmed (source: Salesforce Contact Roles, Avoma call history).
Planned discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams were never scheduled, and the mutual NDA was never finalized (source: prior sweep memory, no follow-on Salesforce tasks or Avoma calls).
Close date 12 Nov 2026 (131 days) is indefensible: Pipeline forecast, Qualified stage, no champion, no Economic Buyer, no buyer engagement in 8+ months, and a known IT organizational blocker.
north_star_verdict.headline: Stalled 8+ months in Qualified with no buyer engagement since October 2025; IT build-vs-buy resistance is unresolved and blocks progression; Economic Buyer unmapped; close date (131 days) is indefensible without immediate IT and senior Procurement engagement.

flags: Co-author a build-vs-buy business case with Ryan Murphy and IT leadership that quantifies the cost, risk, and time-to-market of building vs. buying Zycus. Use Merlin AI (Intake, Agentic AI, ANA in scope) as the differentiation anchor and bring customer references who chose buy-over-build. This directly addresses IT's build preference (the primary blocker per 17 Jun 2026 Next Step) and creates a structured, data-driven path to restarting the evaluation.

Leverage the Gartner recommendation (documented 17 Jun 2026) as external validation and urgency catalyst. Position Zycus as the Gartner-aligned modern platform that addresses the gaps Gartner identified, and use it to justify a formal vendor evaluation track in parallel with any internal build exploration. This turns an external pressure point into a wedge to re-engage the buyer and neutralize IT's resistance.
Single-threaded on Ryan Murphy (Evaluator), who has not engaged in 254 days; Decision Maker Kimberly Blair never engaged; no IT, Finance, or C-level contacts mapped or engaged.
Economic Buyer unknown and unengaged; IT owns procurement technology investments per Ryan Murphy, but no IT budget owner has been identified or engaged.
No champion identified or developed; Ryan Murphy has not demonstrated advocacy or access to decision-makers.
IT build-vs-buy resistance is the primary blocker, but IT has never been engaged and the resistance remains unaddressed.
No quantified ROI case or business justification to counter IT's build preference or validate the $200k investment.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal violates multiple best practices: it has been stalled in Qualified stage for 8+ months with no buyer engagement since the initial discovery call, no champion has been developed, the Economic Buyer is unmapped and unengaged, IT's build-vs-buy resistance is unresolved, and the close date is indefensible. The deal is single-threaded on an Evaluator (Ryan Murphy) who has gone silent, and no quantified ROI case has been built to justify the investment or counter IT's build preference. The deal requires an immediate restart with IT and senior Procurement engagement, or it should be disqualified.

competitive_position.summary: Carhartt faces three competitive forces: (1) IT's strong preference for building custom software over buying vendor platforms, reinforced by existing in-house PO and supplier setup bots; (2) SAP Ariba as a potential vendor alternative; and (3) status quo / do-nothing inertia. The IT build-vs-buy resistance is the primary competitor and the core organizational blocker. Zycus has not yet engaged IT, quantified the ROI case, or positioned Merlin AI as the decisive differentiator that IT cannot cost-effectively build.

competitors.quote: IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying established vendor solutions.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin AI is included in the product scope (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA), and Ryan Murphy expressed strong interest in Merlin AI capabilities on the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call. However, the AI positioning has not been activated: IT has never been shown the Merlin value proposition, no AI-led demo or ROI workshop has been conducted, and the narrative that Merlin AI is the breakthrough capability IT cannot cost-effectively build has not been delivered. The deal is under-positioned on AI because the primary decision-maker (IT) is unengaged and the AI differentiation has not been leveraged to counter the build-vs-buy resistance.

items.quote: Ryan Murphy expressed interest in Zycus iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, and eInvoicing to modernize and automate fragmented manual procurement processes.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy
items.criterion: Unified P2P platform covering contract management, sourcing, supplier management, eProcurement, and eInvoicing

items.quote: Ryan Murphy expressed strong interest in Merlin AI capabilities for intake, negotiation, and analytics (Agentic AI, ANA).
items.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy

items.quote: IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying established vendor solutions.
items.criterion: Build vs buy: IT prefers building custom software over buying vendor platforms

items.quote: No quantified business case, baseline cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount burden, or projected ROI/savings captured on the discovery call.
items.criterion: Quantified ROI case to justify $200k investment and counter IT build preference

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Ryan Murphy expressed strong interest in a unified P2P platform covering contract management, sourcing, supplier management, eProcurement, eInvoicing, and Merlin AI capabilities on the 23 Oct 2025 discovery call. Zycus product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) aligns well with these needs. However, IT's build-vs-buy preference and the lack of a quantified ROI case create misalignment at the organizational level: Zycus has not yet demonstrated that buying is faster, cheaper, and lower-risk than building, especially on AI capabilities. The fit is strong at the functional level (Procurement) but unproven at the decision-making level (IT and Economic Buyer).

hard.ais_why:
• No Avoma calls recorded on the opp; only signal is the SF next step noting Carhartt's 'Procurement Committee continues to develop requirements,' with no AI language.
• Description is blank and there are no transcribed conversations to confirm any prospect-side AI excitement.
• Opp sits at Qualified with steady cadence (last activity May 12) but is committee-led requirements gathering - too early and too quiet on AI to call anything stronger than Curious.
hard.next_step: June 17 Rick:  Gartner engaged, then strongly recommended implementing a new procurement solution. However, we face an internal hurdle with their IT department, which heavily favors building custom software over buying established vendor solutions.

value.quote: IT department heavily favors building custom software over buying established vendor solutions.
value.source: Next Step 17 Jun 2026
value.how_we_win: Position Merlin AI (Agentic AI, ANA, Merlin Intake, negotiation agent) as the breakthrough capability that IT cannot build cost-effectively or quickly; frame the decision as build-vs-buy on AI sophistication and speed to value, not basic P2P functionality; engage IT directly with a Merlin-led demo and ROI case showing that building equivalent AI would take 18+ months and cost more than buying Zycus.

packets.subject: Internal IT build preference (build vs buy)

value.name: Status quo / do-nothing (incremental internal bots)
value.quote: Carhartt has already built in-house PO and supplier setup bots.
value.source: Prior sweep memory
value.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of continuing with fragmented manual processes and incremental bot improvements vs a unified AI-driven P2P platform; show the opportunity cost of slow procurement cycles, manual bottlenecks, and lack of AI-driven insights; make the status quo unacceptable by tying pain to business impact.

value.name: Status quo / do-nothing (incremental internal bots)
value.quote: In-house PO bots and supplier setup bots have been developed to reduce manual effort but are still early in the maturity curve.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy
value.how_we_win: Demonstrate that piecemeal bots cannot deliver the end-to-end automation, supplier collaboration, contract intelligence, and AI-driven negotiation that Zycus provides. Show the hidden cost of maintaining and scaling in-house bots vs. a proven SaaS platform. Anchor with a quantified ROI case that compares current manual cost + bot TCO vs. Zycus full-suite value.

packets.subject: Status quo / do-nothing (incremental internal bots)

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.source: Prior sweep memory
value.how_we_win: Differentiate on Merlin AI capabilities (agentic negotiation, intake automation, advanced analytics) that Ariba lacks; emphasize Zycus AI maturity and P2P depth vs Ariba's ERP-centric, less agile platform; position as the AI-first procurement platform vs Ariba's legacy architecture.

value.name: SAP Ariba
value.quote: SAP Ariba was evaluated but deemed stagnant and not fully meeting Carhartt's modernization needs.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 23 Oct 2025, Ryan Murphy
value.how_we_win: Ariba is out. Position Zycus as the modern alternative Ariba is not: AI-native (Merlin), composable (API-first integration), and purpose-built for mid-market agility vs. SAP's enterprise overhead. Use this as proof that Carhartt has already rejected the legacy incumbent and is looking for innovation.

packets.subject: SAP Ariba

value.detail: The deal has been stalled in Qualified stage for 247 days (8+ months) with no follow-on buyer engagement since the initial discovery call. The planned discovery sessions with P2P and AP teams were never scheduled, and the NDA was never finalized. The close date (12 Nov 2026, 139 days out) is indefensible without an immediate restart and accelerated path through the remaining gates.
value.source: SF stage history (247 days in Qualified); Avoma manifest (no calls since 23 Oct 2025)

value.detail: Carhartt has already built in-house PO and supplier setup bots, which IT may prefer to incrementally improve rather than replace with a vendor platform. This creates inertia and a status-quo bias that reinforces IT's build-over-buy preference.
value.source: Prior sweep memory, Next Step 17 Jun 2026

value.flag: Co-author a build-vs-buy business case with Ryan Murphy and IT leadership that quantifies the cost, risk, and time-to-market of building vs. buying Zycus. Use Merlin AI (Intake, Agentic AI, ANA in scope) as the differentiation anchor and bring customer references who chose buy-over-build. This directly addresses IT's build preference (the primary blocker per 17 Jun 2026 Next Step) and creates a structured, data-driven path to restarting the evaluation.

value.flag: Multi-thread immediately: engage the relevant stakeholder Blair (Senior Director, Corporate Procurement, Ryan's boss, unengaged for 8 months) and IT leadership (likely CIO Katrina Agusti or a direct report who owns technology investments). The deal is single-threaded through Ryan Murphy, who has not re-engaged since Oct 2025. Without senior Procurement and IT buy-in, the deal cannot overcome the build-vs-buy blocker or advance to the relevant stakeholder.

value.flag: Leverage the Gartner recommendation (documented 17 Jun 2026) as external validation and urgency catalyst. Position Zycus as the Gartner-aligned modern platform that addresses the gaps Gartner identified, and use it to justify a formal vendor evaluation track in parallel with any internal build exploration. This turns an external pressure point into a wedge to re-engage the buyer and neutralize IT's resistance.
